_description: Phase 7 attention-head residual cycle deltas between the Regime-B sim oracle (azurelily/IMC/test.py --model attention --seq_length 128 --head_dim 64) and the cycle-accurate RTL TB (tb_{nldpe,azurelily}_attn_head_v2.v), annotated with file:line root causes. Post-2026-04-24 the TBs emit AH_*_STAGES_TOTAL with stage-total durations (T_last_active - T_first_active + 1) OR-aggregated across all W=16 DIMM lanes — see the probe-semantics comment block in each TB. A per-stage delta present here is accepted by run_checks.py (AH latency path) as long as |delta - expected_delta_cycles| <= expected_delta_tolerance.

_schema.stage: linear_qkv | mac_qk | softmax_exp | softmax_norm | mac_sv | e2e
_schema.delta_cycles: RTL - sim (negative = RTL faster than sim's conservative analytical; positive = RTL slower)
_schema.tolerance: allowed wiggle around delta_cycles (per-stage, ~30% of |delta_cycles|, min 100 cyc)
_schema.root_cause: file:line citation of the structural or modelling source of the residual

note: Phase 7.5 (2026-04-24) — sim parallelism fix in azurelily/IMC/peripherals/fpga_fabric.py. Two surgical edits: (1) gemm_log adds intra-row pipelining where next-pass feed overlaps current-pass compute+drain (intra_row_steady=max(feed, compute_chain, drain)); (2) gemm_dsp accepts an n_parallel_outputs parameter so callers (scheduler._run_dimm and _run_linear) can declare the actual hardware DSP-axis parallelism rather than defaulting to the global total_dsp pool — DIMM parallel-row layers and parallel-output FCs use n_parallel_outputs=N and =out_channels respectively, eliminating the dsp_batch>1 serialisation that dominated the pre-fix residuals. Effect on residuals: NL-DPE mac_qk Δ shrinks from -2621 to -2359, mac_sv Δ from -2548 to -2286, e2e from -6122 to -5597 (all small shifts because gemm_log was already fairly close, the intra-row fix saves only the (n-1)·(L-steady) slack). Azure-Lily mac_qk Δ swings from -4374 to +1472 (sim now models 16 parallel dsp_macs instead of 8x serialised batches), mac_sv Δ from -6026 to -1385, e2e Δ from -9091 to +1395 — most stages now lie within ~|1500| cyc, classified modelling_granularity.

deltas.root_cause: Streaming-fill + drain-edge + per-token FSM transition overhead in the 3 fc_top_qkv_streaming arms (fc_verification/rtl/nldpe/fc_top_qkv_streaming.v:20-60). The sim's IMC pipelined-row model (azurelily/IMC/test.py linear_Q phase, 23553.33 ns / 9.7182 ns ~= 2424 cyc) folds the steady-state 183.33 ns/row into a clean N*compute_row count, while the RTL incurs ~2-cyc DPE_A->DPE_B ping-pong handshake per token plus a 26-cyc PACKED_K window for the final drain that gemm_log rolls into the throughput average. Stage-total = T_fc_last_valid - T_fc_first_valid + 1 = 3315 cyc; sim expects 2424; delta +891 dominated by per-token excess ~7 cyc x 128 tokens. Unaffected by the 2026-04-24 sim parallelism fix (linear layer for NL-DPE goes through imc_core.run_gemm, not fpga.gemm_log).

RTL has W=16 hardware lanes (fc_verification/rtl/nldpe_dimm_top_d64_c128.v:29-75, dimm_score_matrix instantiated 16x via dimm_lane[0..15]) producing 16 attention rows in parallel. Stage-total measures the OR-aggregated S_OUTPUT span across all 16 score_inst FSMs — the active mac_qk output stream window is 3069 cyc (each lane drains 8 scores then idles). Sim's gemm_log path (azurelily/IMC/peripherals/fpga_fabric.py:421, post-2026-04-24 intra-row pipelining: total = cycles_per_pass + (n-1)*intra_row_steady) computes 5428 cyc = 52750.00 ns / 9.7182 ns. Pre-fix sim was 5690 cyc; the 2026-04-24 intra-row pipelining edit saved (n-1)*(L-steady) cycles by recognising that the RTL DPE lane physically pipelines feed of pass k+1 with compute+drain of pass k. Residual remains structural — the W=16 hardware-lane parallelism still outperforms the single-lane analytical sim by ~2.36k cyc.

RTL's softmax_approx SM_EXP state (fc_verification/rtl/nldpe_dimm_top_d64_c128.v:437-477, sm_state SM_EXP=3'd2) processes N_PER_LANE=8 rows in 8 cycles per lane via DPE I|exp; with 16 parallel lanes mostly running in lockstep, the OR-aggregated SM_EXP span across all 16 lanes is just 8 cyc. Sim (azurelily/models/attention.py exp layer, 6320.00 ns / 9.7182 ns = 650 cyc) uses a single-lane analytical that scales linearly with N=128. Same parallelism story as mac_qk: sim is conservative under-estimate of W-lane parallelism. Unaffected by 2026-04-24 sim fix (softmax_exp uses dimm_nonlinear, not gemm_log/gemm_dsp).

RTL's softmax_approx SM_NORMALIZE state (fc_verification/rtl/nldpe_dimm_top_d64_c128.v:437-477, sm_state SM_NORMALIZE=3'd3) completes per-lane log-fusion in 10 cyc using 16 parallel lanes (N_PER_LANE+2 cycles = 10). Sim's norm layer (azurelily/models/attention.py norm layer, 3653.33 ns / 9.7182 ns = 376 cyc) uses a single-lane analytical with the [log-softmax fusion] sum=10.79 + log=75.01 + sub=10.88 pJ/row formula serially over 128 rows. Conservative under-estimate of W-lane parallelism. Unaffected by 2026-04-24 sim fix.

RTL's wsum stage (fc_verification/rtl/nldpe_dimm_top_d64_c128.v dimm_lane[0..15] wsum_inst, ws_state WS_OUTPUT=4'd5) drains weighted-sum outputs across all 16 lanes in a 2791-cyc OR-aggregated WS_OUTPUT span. Sim's gemm_log mac_sv (azurelily/models/attention.py mac_sv layer, 49340.00 ns / 9.7182 ns = 5077 cyc, post-2026-04-24 intra-row pipelining) uses single-lane analytical with K_id=2, 16 effective parallel = 32 effective DPEs, but the analytical formula serializes the per-output reduction. Pre-fix sim was 5339 cyc; the intra-row pipelining edit saved (n-1)*(L-steady) cycles. RTL benefits from W=16 lane broadcast; sim is conservative under-estimate.

Stage-total e2e = T_data_out_last_valid - T_x_first_valid + 1 = 8358 cyc (full pipeline duration). Sum of per-stage residuals: linear_qkv (+891) + mac_qk (-2359) + softmax_exp (-642) + softmax_norm (-366) + mac_sv (-2286) = -4762; e2e measured -5597 (small additional offset from RTL pipelining stage boundaries — DIMM start can begin draining mid-FC drain, gemm_log strict-serial composition cannot match this). Dominant negative term is W=16 lane parallelism speedup in mac_qk + mac_sv + softmax = -5653 cyc, partially offset by FC streaming overhead (+891). Classification structural — same root cause as the per-stage structural deltas: RTL's W-lane parallelism outperforms sim's conservative analytical lower-bound. The 2026-04-24 intra-row pipelining edit shifted e2e from -6122 to -5597 (525 cyc saved, matching the sum of mac_qk and mac_sv intra-row savings).

Streaming-fill + drain-edge + per-token FSM transition overhead in the 3 azurelily_fc_128_64 arms (fc_verification/rtl/azurelily/azurelily_fc_128_64.v). The sim's pipelined model (azurelily/IMC/test.py linear_Q phase post-fix, 45506.56 ns / 11.3766 ns ~= 4000 cyc) folds steady-state into a clean N*compute_row count; the stage-total RTL FC-stream span is 4458 cyc = T_fc_last_valid - T_fc_first_valid + 1. Per-token excess ~3.5 cyc x 128 tokens = +458 cyc, matching the measurement. Unaffected by 2026-04-24 sim fix (Azure-Lily linear layer routes through imc_core.run_gemm, not fpga.gemm_dsp).

Stage-total = T_mac_qk_last_out - T_mac_qk_first_out + 1 = 2287 cyc (the OR-aggregated mac_qk_inst.out_valid span across all 16 lanes; fc_verification/rtl/azurelily_dimm_top_d64_c128.v:104). Sim's gemm_dsp path (azurelily/IMC/peripherals/fpga_fabric.py:181 with n_parallel_outputs=N=128 from scheduler._run_dimm post-2026-04-24) computes 815 cyc = 9270.00 ns / 11.3766 ns. Pre-2026-04-24 sim with default total_dsp=16 produced dsp_batch=ceil(128/16)=8 (8x serialisation) → 6661 cyc. The 2026-04-24 fix sets dsp_batch=1 by passing n_parallel_outputs=N (DIMM RTL has W=16 parallel lanes that effectively act as N=128 parallel outputs at this row-major mapping). The residual +1472 reflects the FSM-iteration overhead in the AL DIMM-top: the outer FSM serialises 128 row iterations (state==S_FEED_QK with mac_count gating) while sim models the lanes as fully parallel. Classification modelling_granularity — sim's intra-wave-pipelining model (first wave + (wave-1)*intra_steady) is correct for parallel-output dataflow; the residual is the OUTER row-iteration FSM overhead which is not captured by gemm_dsp's steady-state interval.

Stage-total softmax_exp = T_softmax_in_last - T_softmax_in_first + 1 = 2287 cyc (OR-aggregated softmax_inst.valid input pulse span across all 16 lanes, mapping to clb_softmax S_LOAD duration where exp_val is computed combinationally and accumulated; fc_verification/rtl/azurelily_dimm_top_d64_c128.v:230-244, 269-282). Sim's softmax_exp (azurelily/models/attention.py exp layer, 2133.33 ns / 11.3766 ns = 188 cyc) uses an analytical formula that assumes parallel softmax across W=16 lanes (total_dimm_dpes=16) and ignores the input-rate bottleneck. RTL's S_LOAD duration is bottlenecked by mac_qk's K=16 cycles/output throughput: 128 scores × 16 cyc/score = 2048 cyc + ~239 cyc S_INV/transition overhead = 2287 cyc. The +2099 residual reflects the sim's under-modelling of the input-rate gating between mac_qk and clb_softmax — a fundamentally analytical model coarseness, not a structural divergence (the sim's combined softmax_exp+softmax_norm = 938 cyc is much closer to RTL's combined 2414 cyc). Unaffected by 2026-04-24 sim fix.

Stage-total softmax_norm = T_softmax_norm_last - T_softmax_norm_first + 1 = 127 cyc (OR-aggregated softmax_inst.valid_n output span across all 16 lanes, mapping to clb_softmax S_NORM duration; fc_verification/rtl/azurelily_dimm_top_d64_c128.v:287-294). Each clb_softmax runs S_NORM for N=128 cycles, but lanes overlap heavily because they all start S_LOAD together and finish ~simultaneously, so the wall-clock OR-aggregated span is ~127 cyc. Sim's softmax_norm (azurelily/models/attention.py norm layer, 8533.33 ns / 11.3766 ns = 750 cyc) uses single-lane analytical that under-models W=16 hardware-lane parallelism — same conservative-under-estimate story as mac_qk / mac_sv. Classification structural — RTL outperforms sim's analytical lower-bound by W-lane parallelism. Unaffected by 2026-04-24 sim fix.

RTL has W=16 dsp_mac hardware lanes (fc_verification/rtl/azurelily_dimm_top_d64_c128.v:125 mac_sv dsp_mac with K=32, instantiated W=16 times via al_lane[0..15]) producing 16 weighted-sum outputs in parallel. Stage-total = T_mac_sv_last_out - T_mac_sv_first_out + 1 = 65 cyc (OR-aggregated mac_sv_inst.out_valid span across all 16 lanes; each lane's mac_sv consumes attn_valid pulses N=128 times with K=32 cycles per output, so 4 outputs per lane). Sim's gemm_dsp path (azurelily/models/attention.py mac_sv layer, 16493.33 ns / 11.3766 ns = 1450 cyc, post-2026-04-24 with n_parallel_outputs=N=64=d_head) uses parallel-output formula. Pre-2026-04-24 sim was 6091 cyc with dsp_batch=8; the 2026-04-24 fix passes n_parallel_outputs=N=d_head=64 to gemm_dsp giving dsp_batch=ceil(M/64)=2 (since n_first=False because M=128>N=64). This still leaves a 2× wave serialisation that explains why mac_sv sim doesn't fully collapse. RTL outperforms sim because the FSM consumes 128 attn_valid pulses serially with 16-lane parallelism — sim is now closer (Δ -1385 vs pre-fix -6026). Classification structural for the residual W-lane parallelism advantage that remains.

Stage-total e2e = T_data_out_last_valid - T_x_first_valid + 1 = 8598 cyc (full pipeline duration). Sum of per-stage residuals: linear_qkv (+458) + mac_qk (+1472) + softmax_exp (+2099) + softmax_norm (-623) + mac_sv (-1385) = +2021; e2e measured +1395 (sim now slightly OVER-estimates by ~626 cyc due to inter-stage pipeline overlap that the strict-serial sim composition does not capture). Dominant terms post-2026-04-24 fix: softmax_exp input-rate bottleneck (+2099) and mac_qk FSM-iteration overhead (+1472), partially offset by mac_sv (-1385) and softmax_norm (-623). Classification modelling_granularity — the sim now produces a positive E2E residual (sim is conservative on softmax_exp / mac_qk's outer FSM; RTL is faster on softmax_norm / mac_sv via W-lane parallelism); the cancellation makes the e2e residual small relative to the stage sums.
